'''Ank Kit'aar''' was a celestial body<ref name="CRO" /> located in the [[Ank Kit'aar system/Legends|Ank Kit'aar system]],<ref name="TEA" /> a part of the [[Druess sector/Legends|Druess sector]]<ref name="CRO" /> in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. Not long after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Ank Kit'aar was noted in [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[General/Legends|general]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken's]] [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives as the last known location of [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Kerri Lessev/Legends|Kerri Lessev]], an agent of the [[Destabilization]] branch of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Intelligence]] service tasked with preventing high-ranking Imperial personnel from posing a threat to the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Lessev was also secretly an ally of the Alliance seeking to weaken the Empire by causing the elimination of some of the most competent and capable Imperial figures.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ank Kit'aar was created by [[David R. Tulo, Jr.]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Ank Kit'aar [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square O-15.<ref name="TEA" />
